text.title: requirements-documentation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for requirements-documentation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using "should", "may", or "might" in an SRS that mandates "shall".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: RFC 2119 and IEEE 830 conventions differ; an agent trained on RFC documents writes MAY/SHOULD in a "shall"-only SRS. Lint with the conformance schema; reject modal-verb drift.

failure-mode.description: Standard hallucination — agent produces "IEEE 830-2025" sections that do not exist.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the standard in the prompt and reject any structural claim not present in the schema file; use the srs-conformance.yaml as ground truth.

failure-mode.description: EARS pattern collapse — agent defaults all FRs to Ubiquitous.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a per-requirement EARS classification call before phrasing; require the agent to name the pattern before writing the sentence.

failure-mode.description: Approval block fabricated — agent populates approval table with names and dates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Approval table must be a stub the human fills; an agent-populated approval block is a forged audit record.

failure-mode.description: Section-completeness theatre — agent fills every section with "N/A".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force "drop or fill" — emit nothing if no content applies; track section coverage as a metric, not section presence.

failure-mode.description: Glossary forks — agent redefines a term inline rather than reading the shared glossary.md.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Provide glossary.md in context for every populate call; forbid in-doc definitions that conflict with the glossary.

failure-mode.description: Standard hallucination. Agent produces "IEEE 830-2025" sections that do not exist.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the standard in the prompt and reject any structural claim not present in the schema file. Use the srs-conformance.yaml as ground truth.

failure-mode.description: EARS pattern collapse. Agent defaults all FRs to Ubiquitous.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a per-requirement EARS classification call before phrasing. Require the agent to name the pattern before writing the sentence.

failure-mode.description: Modal-verb drift. RFC 2119 vs IEEE 830 conventions differ; agent trained on RFCs writes "MAY", "SHOULD" in an SRS that mandates "shall" only.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lint with the schema; reject may|should|might. Ban these words at linting time.

failure-mode.description: Glossary forks. Agent redefines an existing term inline rather than reading the glossary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Provide glossary.md in context for every populate call and forbid in-doc definitions.

failure-mode.description: Approval-block fabrication. Agent emitting a populated approval table with names + dates is forging an audit record.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Approval table must be a stub the human fills. Approval block must remain empty for agent-driven workflows.

failure-mode.description: Section-completeness theatre. Agent fills every section with "N/A" rather than dropping it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force "drop or fill" — emit nothing if no content applies; track section-coverage as a metric.

failure-mode.description: NFR template-numbness. Models reproduce IEEE 830 §5.5 categories verbatim without adapting to the project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run a tailoring pass: prune categories that do not apply (e.g., no portability for an internal-only web app).

failure-mode.description: Re-baseline silence. Agent edits an Approved doc in place.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Status must be a CI-checked invariant: any change to an Approved file must bump version and reset status to Draft.

failure-mode.description: PDF render drift. Re-rendering yields a binary diff (font kerning, generated date).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin pandoc version, supply a deterministic timestamp (SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH), and diff via pdftotext.

failure-mode.description: Traceability matrix overload. A 5,000-row matrix is unreadable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Auto-generate one matrix per top-level capability instead of one project-wide.
